title: The Great Depression
description: What caused the banking crisis of 1929? What were governmental responses to the Great Depression?

assessment_items.question_content:
Why is October 29th, 1929 called **Black Tuesday**?

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: There was no ash storm that came out of Albany in 1929.

**Solution:**
[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: On that day, stock prices plummeted, signaling the beginning of the Great Depression.
A violent and terrible ash storm came out of Albany and blackened all of New York City, convincing New Yorkers that the world was ending.
The _HMS Cassiopeia_, a British ship, pulled into Boston Harbor, bringing with it the first-ever case of Black Plague to hit the shores of North America.

assessment_items.question_content:
Why did shantytowns, disparagingly called **Hoovervilles** over President Hoover’s perceived inaction, emerge across the nation?

[[☃ radio 3]]
hints: It was President Eisenhower who signed the National Interstate Highways Act into law in 1955.

**Solution:**
[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Out-of-work Americans, unable to pay mortgages or rent, were evicted and made homeless.  They built the shantytowns in public parks and vacant lots.
Hoover’s support of the National Interstate Highways Act destroyed homes and displaced communities.
They were protest communities that sprung up in the wake of the Immigration Act of 1924.

assessment_items.question_content:
President **Calvin Coolidge**’s approach to the American economy can be best described as



[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Coolidge was no protectionist; even Hoover, his successor, signed the Smoot-Hawley Tariff with great hesitation.

**Solution:**
[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: laissez-faire; he limited federal regulation of the economy, cut spending, and lowered income tax rates.
socialist; he nationalized many private businesses and redistributed wealth to the impoverished.
protectionist; he was strongly in favor of tariffs that placed high premiums on foreign goods.

assessment_items.question_content:
The “Three Rs” of the **New Deal** are:




[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: The New Deal did not focus on eliminating government programs.

**Solution:**
[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: **Relief** for the unemployed, **Recovery** of the economy, and **Reform** of capitalism through regulation
**Ration** resources for communities, **Regulate** free markets, and **Repeal** the 18th Amendment.
**Reinstate** government protections for small businesses, **Remove** ineffective government programs, and **Raise** the minimum wage for workers.

assessment_items.question_content:
What did the **Glass-Steagall Act of 1932** accomplish?



[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: The Glass-Steagall Act did not deny consumers the right to an insured deposit.

**Solution:**
[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: It separated investment banks from commercial banks and established the Federal Deposit Insurance Corporation.
It combined both investment banks and commercial banks and denied consumers the right to an insured deposit.
It established the system of home mortgaging that is still in place today.

assessment_items.question_content:
The Banking Act of 1933, also known as the **Glass-Steagall Act**, established the Federal Deposit Insurance Corporation (FDIC), which




[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: U.S. citizens are not required to have a bank account, with a major commercial bank or otherwise.

**Solution:**
[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: protected depositors from financial losses when banks failed.
protected banks from failure by ensuring that the government would bail them out if necessary.
required American taxpayers to establish an account with a commercial bank.

assessment_items.question_content:
The **Hawley-Smoot Tariff Act**, intended to protect American industry, had the effect of 




[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: The Hawley-Smoot act did not decrease tariffs on imported goods.

**Solution:**
[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: raising the prices of imported goods, initiating a trade war between the United States and Europe and making the Great Depression worse.
decreasing prices on American goods exported to Europe, leading to an unbalanced global economy.
instantly ending the Great Depression by reducing the supply of manufactured goods and stabilizing prices.

assessment_items.question_content:
The first **hundred days** of Franklin Delano Roosevelt’s first term were marked by
[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Roosevelt was a popular and well-regarded reformist governor.

**Solution:**
[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: a prolonged burst of legislative activity intended to provide relief for the Great Depression
Idleness, due to the lack of government funds to stimulate change
Riots in New York City; as Governor, he was viewed as illegitimate, and once he ascended to the presidency it only got more pronounced.

assessment_items.question_content:
The **Civilian Conservation Corps** provided unemployment relief by



[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: The CCC didn’t issue small-business loans.

**Solution:**
[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: giving jobs to young unmarried men on government-owned land, planting trees, fighting fires, and building bridges, roads, and dams.
Sending housing and food stipends to all unemployed citizens
Offering loans to unemployed citizens to start their own businesses

assessment_items.question_content:
The **National Industrial Recovery Act** was a New Deal program that 


[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: DARPA was created in 1958 under the Eisenhower administration.

**Solution:**
[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: established the Public Works Administration and National Recovery Administration, creating building projects and setting industry codes of conduct
Provided tax cuts to the auto industry in order to boost production and wages for workers
created DARPA, the Defense Advanced Research Projects Agency that would go on to create the Internet

assessment_items.question_content:
The **Agricultural Adjustment Act** helped drive up the price of agricultural goods by



[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: The Agricultural Adjustment Act had no provisions against pesticides.

**Solution:**
[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: paying farmers **not** to grow crops and reduce output.
Re-zoning commercial property, allowing farmers to grow more crops.
Prohibited farmers from using pesticides, thus decreasing crop output.

assessment_items.question_content:
On top of the already dire financial situation caused by the bank crisis, how were farmers in the High Plains affected by **a natural disaster** during the Great Depression?





[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: farmers in the High Plains were not affected by a flood during this time period.

**Solution:**
[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: A terrible drought that lasted throughout the 1930s led to a series of dust storms known as the Dust Bowl.
A series of unforeseen earthquakes in the early 1930s decimated farms and destroyed equipment.
An unprecedented amount of rain in the High Plains led to the Great Flood of 1933, causing farmers to lose most of their crops.

assessment_items.question_content:
What did the **Social Security Act** put into place?






[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: The Social Security Act is not associated with prohibiting discrimination based on socio-economic status.

**Solution:**
[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: a trust fund, paid for by payroll taxes, to support retirees over the age of 65, as well as Americans with long-term disabilities
a trust fund, paid for by payroll taxes, to support lower-income citizens with aid like food stamps
a law prohibiting public institutions from discriminating against employees (or prospective employees) based on socio-economic status

assessment_items.question_content:
Why did President Franklin Roosevelt want to expand the Supreme Court of the United States beyond nine justices?







[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: The justices were generally not receptive to Roosevelt’s New Deal programs.

**Solution:**
[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: He wanted to “pack the court” with liberal justices who would rule in favor of his New Deal programs.
He thought one of the justices was biased and wanted to add another justice to balance the votes.
Since the justices had been so receptive to his New Deal programs, Roosevelt wanted to increase the number of justices on the bench so his programs could be approved in even higher numbers.

assessment_items.question_content:
What did economist **John Maynard Keynes** believe about deficit spending?



[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Keynes did not believe the government should be privatized.

**Solution:**
[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Government policies that put cash in the hands of citizens would lead to greater purchasing of consumer goods, which would in turn enable business owners to hire more workers to produce more goods.

Government spending would only deepen the hole in the deficit created during the Great Depression. In order to climb out of it, Keynes suggested that the private sector and charitable organizations grant money to the federal government.
Government policies that put cash in the hands of citizens would lead to more money spent in the private sector. Because of this, Keynes believed the government should largely be privatized.
